and it has no inclination towards salvation and is therefore inimical

JUPITER
Strictly speaking Jupiter is not inimical to any other planet.
The relationship between Jupiter (preceptor) and Mercury (child
or Student) are like that of a master and student. A master
although may chide the student at times, still he is not inimical to
the student. For instance, if in a class-room an intelligent student
poses an inconvenient question or a question which the teacher is
incapable of answering, the teacher may chide the student, just to
silence the student. But in the heart of his heart, the teacher will
be appreciative of such student. A combination of Jupiter and
Mercury gives Extraordinary intelligence to the native because

FUNDAMENTAL OF RAO'S SYSTEM OF NADI ASlROLOGY 33
Guru Buclhi Visheshita has Jupiter cannot like Moon because of
the wavering and of Moon. Jupiter likes Saturn because work is
guided by Jupiter.
